body:
## Rationale

`api/meta/[...path].js:189` handles `path === '/stats'` but NOT the sub-page `/stats/gear-insights`. The sitemap declares this page at `api/sitemap.js:395` (priority 0.9) but crawlers see the generic fallback title.

GSC queries like "what gear do metal drummers use stats" and "most popular drum brand metal" would rank better with a keyword-matched title/description.

## Fix

**File:** `api/meta/[...path].js`

Add immediately after the existing `/stats` block (after line ~196):

```js
if (path === '/stats/gear-insights') {
  return {
    title: `Metal Drummer Gear Stats & Insights — Brand Usage Data | ${SITE_NAME}`,
    description: 'Real data: which drum brands, cymbals, and pedals are most popular among 60+ pro metal drummers. Usage percentages, trends, and brand breakdowns.',
    image: `${BASE_URL}/images/og/stats-preview.png`,
    type: 'website',
    url: `${BASE_URL}/stats/gear-insights`,
  };
}
```

## Verify

1. `curl -A "Googlebot" https://metalforge.io/api/meta/stats/gear-insights` → title contains "Gear Stats"
2. `curl -A "Googlebot" https://metalforge.io/api/meta/stats` → still returns original stats title (no regression)
3. Description < 160 chars ✓
4. `curl https://metalforge.io/sitemap.xml | grep stats/gear-insights` → present in sitemap
5. No conflict with parent `/stats` handler (exact match, different path)

## Done

- [ ] Case block added for `path === '/stats/gear-insights'` in `api/meta/[...path].js`
title: SEO: /stats/gear-insights sub-page missing SSR meta — serves generic fallback
